title: Nomarr Architecture Decision Record
description: Schema for ADR markdown files in artifacts/decisions/. An ADR captures a single architectural decision with structured metadata and required discussion sections.

title.description: ADR title extracted from the H1 heading.
examples: ADR-001: Use ONNX Runtime for ML Inference

metadata.description: Bold-formatted key-value pairs between the title and first section heading.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the ADR.

tags.description: Comma-separated topic tags. At least one tag is required.

source_log.description: Optional reference to the agent log entry that motivated this decision.

supersedes.description: Optional list of ADR identifiers this decision supersedes. Comma-separated in markdown.

sections.description: H2-level sections containing the decision narrative. Standard sections appear in a fixed order; extra sections are allowed between Consequences and References.

Context.description: Why this decision is needed. Describes forces, constraints, and background.

Decision.description: What was decided. States the chosen approach clearly.

Consequences.description: What follows from the decision — both positive and negative impacts.

References.description: Optional section listing related documents, issues, or links. Always appears last when present.

additionalProperties.description: Custom H2 sections are allowed (e.g., 'Alternatives Considered', 'Migration Plan'). They appear after Consequences and before References.

section_ordering.description: Advisory: sections should appear in the order Context → Decision → Consequences → (custom sections) → References. The parser does not enforce order but the generator emits sections in this sequence.

word_count_advisory.description: Advisory: the combined word count across Context, Decision, and Consequences sections should be at least 100 words. ADRs below this threshold may lack sufficient justification. Tools emit a warning but do not block creation.
